title: Talc & Asbestos Litigation Science - Week of July 2, 2026
executive_summary: Six papers matched the stream query this week; two bear on litigation science. Both concern attribution rather than treatment: one quantifies the ovarian-cancer burden attributable to occupational asbestos exposure across Asia, the other establishes a cohort tracking progression from benign asbestos-associated pleural inflammation to mesothelioma. Together they strengthen the exposure-to-disease evidence base on both the gynecological and pleural endpoints. The four excluded papers were bycatch of the query's broad exposure and cancer terms - a laryngeal cancer burden study, a talc pleurodesis technique trial, a carbon-nanotube exposure framework, and a mesothelioma diagnostic case report.
